name: A process has exited
applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 R2 and later; Windows 7 and later
definition: Event ID 4689 records process termination and completes host process-lifetime timelines.
trigger_scenarios: Windows logs it when an audited process exits.

key_fields.explanation: Join to 4688, accounting for PID reuse.
The security context of the ending process.

false_positives: Routine process termination is high volume.
Short-lived installers and scripts can create bursts.
detection_notes: Use 4689 to investigate T1070.004 cleanup only when a suspicious 4688 process exits immediately after deletion, log clearing, or artifact creation. The value is its duration and sequence, not the termination alone.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4689
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Process, ProcessId
spl_snippet: index=wineventlog EventCode=4689 | table _time, host, Process_Name, Process_ID
